{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is whether saracatinib promotes oncogenesis. In the MEDCORP documents, Document [6] specifically discusses saracatinib as a SRC kinase inhibitor. It states that in an orthotopic xenograft HCC (hepatocellular carcinoma) model, saracatinib inhibited lung metastasis without affecting the primary tumor. This indicates that saracatinib suppresses metastasis, not promotes it. The mechanism described involves blocking downstream signals such as FAK and Stat3, which are involved in cancer progression. There is no evidence in the provided documents suggesting that saracatinib promotes oncogenesis. Instead, the data show it inhibits metastasis. Therefore, saracatinib does not promote oncogenesis.", "answer_choice": "B"}
